purpose: Pure backoff-ladder math, incomplete-contact-set computation, and the state machine governing when a contact's profile is re-requested (active / answered-incomplete / given-up), plus the same-file idb-keyval CRUD adapter for the persisted per-contact schedule. No React/NDK import surface — every stateful input (clock, contact list, cache snapshot, rng) is an explicit parameter.

public_api.name: ProfileScheduleState
public_api.description: The three states a schedule can be in. Only 'active' schedules are ever returned by computeDue.

public_api.name: ProfileSchedule
public_api.signature: type ProfileSchedule = { pubkeyHex: string; attempts: number; nextAttemptAt: number; state: ProfileScheduleState; firstAttemptAt: number; lastResetAt: number | null }
public_api.description: Persisted per-contact record, keyed by lowercased pubkeyHex. Extends the 4-field shape sketched in verification.json's VQ-S02-010 with two additional epoch-second fields (firstAttemptAt, lastResetAt) — see the file header doc and this story's post-impl verification append for why: the 30-day give-up ceiling and the <=1/24h D4 reset rate-limit each need a timestamp anchor, and deriving either from attempts+nextAttemptAt alone is not reliable across resets, jitter, or clock-clamp. Both are opaque to callers outside this module; story 05 (watcher) consumes the type only through the exported functions below, never by hand-constructing or pattern-matching individual fields.

public_api.name: RandomSource
public_api.description: Injectable jitter source returning a value in [0,1). Defaults to Math.random in every function; tests inject a fixed sequence for deterministic jitter-bound assertions.

public_api.name: LADDER_HOURS
public_api.description: The nominal (pre-jitter) backoff rungs in hours, exported so tests assert against the same constant rather than re-deriving it.

public_api.name: CAP_HOURS
public_api.description: The interval every attempt beyond the ladder's length repeats at.

public_api.name: JITTER_FRACTION
public_api.description: ±20% jitter bound (D2).

public_api.name: GIVE_UP_CEILING_SECONDS
public_api.description: AC-PROF-11c's 30-day ceiling, in seconds.

public_api.name: RESET_RATE_LIMIT_SECONDS
public_api.description: AC-PROF-11's <=1 D4 reset per contact per 24h.

public_api.name: nominalIntervalHours
public_api.description: Pure ladder lookup for the Nth (1-based) attempt, capped at CAP_HOURS beyond the ladder's length.

public_api.name: createInitialSchedule
public_api.signature: createInitialSchedule(pubkeyHex: string, nowSec: number, rng?: RandomSource): ProfileSchedule
public_api.description: First-ever schedule for a newly-incomplete contact: attempts=1, nextAttemptAt = nowSec + jittered 1h (AC-PROF-1's 'first fire after 1h, not immediately'), state='active'.

public_api.name: advance
public_api.signature: advance(schedule: ProfileSchedule, nowSec: number, rng?: RandomSource): ProfileSchedule
public_api.description: Called after an active schedule's fire went out. Increments attempts and schedules the next rung (AC-PROF-1/11), UNLESS nowSec - schedule.firstAttemptAt has reached GIVE_UP_CEILING_SECONDS, in which case it transitions to 'given-up' (AC-PROF-11c) instead of scheduling further. No-op (returns schedule unchanged) if state is not 'active'.

public_api.name: markAnsweredIncomplete
public_api.description: Pure transition to the 'answered-incomplete' terminal state (AC-PROF-11a) — called by story 04's receive path when a gate-passing, non-malformed announce nonetheless leaves avatar empty.

public_api.name: applyReachabilitySignal
public_api.signature: applyReachabilitySignal(schedule: ProfileSchedule, nowSec: number, rng?: RandomSource): ProfileSchedule
public_api.description: The single D4 handler shared by both reset-on-activity (AC-PROF-11, from 'active') and re-arm-from-terminal (AC-PROF-11a/11c, from 'answered-incomplete'/'given-up') — resets to the 1h floor and 'active', rate-limited: a no-op if lastResetAt is non-null and less than RESET_RATE_LIMIT_SECONDS ago. Never called for a profile-announce receipt (D4 excludes those; the caller, not this function, filters by rumor kind).

public_api.name: isCompletingAnnounce
public_api.signature: isCompletingAnnounce(params: { lwwWon: boolean; avatarNonNull: boolean }): boolean
public_api.description: Pure predicate for 'this announce should clear the schedule' — true iff the LWW write landed AND the resulting avatar is non-null. Story 04 calls this, then deleteSchedule on true; scheduler holds no receive-path logic itself.

public_api.name: computeDue
public_api.description: Filters to schedules with state==='active' && nowSec >= nextAttemptAt && (nowSec - firstAttemptAt) < GIVE_UP_CEILING_SECONDS. Never returns an 'answered-incomplete' or 'given-up' entry (VQ-S02-010), and never leaks a still-'active' schedule whose give-up ceiling has already elapsed but which advance() has not yet transitioned to 'given-up' (Codex remediation, VQ-S02-015).

public_api.name: ContactSnapshot
public_api.signature: type ContactSnapshot = { pubkeyHex: string; archived: boolean }
public_api.description: Minimal injected view of a contact — the watcher (story 05) maps its own contacts.ts read into this shape; scheduler.ts never imports contacts.ts.

public_api.name: ProfileCacheSnapshot
public_api.signature: type ProfileCacheSnapshot = { pubkeyHex: string; avatarNonNull: boolean }
public_api.description: Minimal injected view of a contactCache.ts entry; scheduler.ts never imports contactCache.ts.

public_api.name: computeIncompleteSet
public_api.signature: computeIncompleteSet(contacts: ContactSnapshot[], cache: ProfileCacheSnapshot[], ownPubkeyHex: string): string[]
public_api.description: Sec 3.1: a contact (excluding own pubkey and archived contacts, and anything not in the injected contact list) is incomplete when it has no cache entry or a cache entry with a null avatar.

public_api.name: computeContactsNeedingNewSchedule
public_api.description: Stage-1 review remediation (integration-hazard closer): computeIncompleteSet is schedule-state-blind, so a naive watcher doing 'for each incomplete contact, createInitialSchedule if none active' would resurrect a terminal ('answered-incomplete'/'given-up') contact by minting a fresh active schedule on the next sweep, re-opening the permanent-loop hole AC-PROF-11a/11c close at the state-machine layer. Returns the subset of incompleteSet with NO existing schedule at all (active OR terminal) — the watcher calls createInitialSchedule ONLY for these pubkeys, holding no terminal-exclusion logic of its own. Case-folds both sides on the join; deduped, lowercased output.

public_api.name: clampScheduleOnLoad
public_api.description: AC-PROF-1's backwards-clock-jump guard: clamps nextAttemptAt down to nowSec + CAP_HOURS*3600 when it exceeds that bound. PURE — never persists; applied by every store read, which is responsible for persisting the clamp durably (see loadSchedule/loadAllSchedules, VQ-S02-015).

public_api.name: saveSchedule
public_api.description: Upsert, case-folding pubkeyHex on both the store key and the persisted record.

public_api.name: loadSchedule
public_api.signature: loadSchedule(pubkeyHex: string, nowSec?: number): Promise<ProfileSchedule | undefined>
public_api.description: Case-folds the lookup key; applies clampScheduleOnLoad before returning. Codex remediation (VQ-S02-015): when the clamp actually lowers nextAttemptAt relative to the stored value, the clamped record is persisted back via saveSchedule before returning, so a subsequent reload at an unchanged (still-behind) clock returns the SAME clamped value instead of re-deriving a fresh, further-forward now+24h on every reload.

public_api.name: loadAllSchedules
public_api.description: Every persisted schedule, each clamped via clampScheduleOnLoad. Codex remediation (VQ-S02-015): mirrors loadSchedule's durable-clamp persistence — every entry the clamp actually lowers is written back to the store before this function returns.

public_api.name: deleteSchedule
public_api.description: Case-folds the key; called on a completing announce (isCompletingAnnounce===true) or on contact archive/remove.

public_api.name: clearAllSchedulesForTests
public_api.description: Test-only full-store reset, mirroring nonceStore.ts's clearAllNonces / pendingIntent.ts's clearPendingIntentsForTests precedent. This module holds no module-scope mutable state beyond the idb-keyval store itself, so this is the only reset needed.

owned_data: The ProfileSchedule record shape and its state machine
